observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare adjusting entries in the books of accounts to reclassify the Due From GOCCs amounting to ₱59.95 million unto Deposits on Letters of Credit account; and
Henceforth, review all journal entries to ensure that transactions are properly recorded and reflective of actual results of the operation to achieve fairness in the presentation of Financial Statements and avoid the possibility of misleading information.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to reconcile the accounts of the noted deficiencies and prepare an appropriate entry to record the adjustments. If a particular account had no more specific purpose, return immediately to the source funding Agency. Henceforth, always ensure that financial transactions are well accounted for and sufficiently disclosed to assure reliability on the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Local Health Board to submit the list of frontline HRHs and public HWs, including BHWs, in relation to AHDP and SRA grant.
HRMO to submit the authority of HRHs and public HWs to physically report in the workstation on the period duration of the subject benefits, including the individual DTRs.
MHO to submit copies of medical journals to support the claims of SRA and provide a written justification for the noted inconsistency on the actual number of duty dates. Otherwise, the total amount of benefits will be disallowed in the audit.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation justifying why the above-mentioned guidelines in granting Active Hazard Duty Pay have not adhered. Henceforth, always ensure that applicable policies appropriate in dealing with the affairs of the LGU shall be observed to avoid possible suspension or disallowance in the audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the immediate liquidation in full of outstanding fund transfers received from DOH CHD – Caraga. Henceforth, strict adherence to the provisions of Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 is enjoined.

We recommended that Management:
Create a Disposal and Appraisal Committee to facilitate the disposal of those unserviceable properties.
Impose a timeline for the disposal of the Municipal Government's unserviceable properties to ensure that the said undertaking be accomplished within the year.
Further, direct the Inventory Committee to complete the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and submit the same to the Appraisal Committee to determine the appraised value of the said properties.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC thru its Secretariat to require the contracted supplier to submit certification coming from the Insurance Commission specific to the procurement at hand. Nevertheless, always ensure to require the subject certification on any subsequent surety or bonds provided by a given supplier or contractor.
We recommended that Management immediately remit the remaining balances of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) to the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended that Management:
Properly addressed the issues and concerns that hindered the implementation of these projects so that PPAs can all be implemented and carried out as planned; and
Review the balance of the continuing appropriation for this fund to determine savings from completed projects. Further, evaluate and re-assess the PPAs if still responsive to the priority needs of the constituents of the Municipality, otherwise reprogram the fund consistent with Paragraph 2, Section 322 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management seek the required authority from the Sangguniang concerned before any charges could be made against lump-sum appropriations, in adherence to Section 22.c of Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Memorandum No. 2010-014 adopting the Supreme Court ruling per GR No. 175527, to support the legality of the transactions.

We recommended that Management direct MDRRMO to stop charging ineligible expenditures from the LDRRM Fund. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance to Section 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and Section 21 of RA No. 10121 in the utilization thereof.

We recommended that Management instruct the concerned HRMO personnel to strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take-home pay for government employees to boost the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned HRMO personnel to properly review the applications for monetization prior to processing the same. Hence, ensure strict compliance with the laws, rules, and regulations on the monetization of leave credits, specifically Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 29

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant not to charge and pay similar-like expenditures using Municipal funds but instead held the negligent personnel liable thereto.
Remind the Sangguniang Bayan not to appropriate government funds to pay penalty-related expenditures.
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to religiously adhere to applicable laws, rules, and regulations by acquiring the required permits and licenses to avoid incurring this type of expenditure.

We recommended that Management direct all Bids and Awards Committee personnel to strictly follow the prescribed posting requirements to enhance transparency and accountability of the government's procurement transactions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the:

Municipal Treasurer to expedite the processing of transfer of title of lands in favor of the Municipality to avoid any future land disputes; and

Municipal Accountant to ensure that all future acquisitions of land are properly supported with necessary documents.
observations.reason: No actions were taken

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to 

Issue demand letters to notify the concerned Officials and Employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If the settlement was not received, considering that they have been notified, withhold from their salaries the whole amount of the cash advance; and

Strictly monitor the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof and to avoid an undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: No actions were taken

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant to:

Issue demand letters to notify the concerned Officials and Employees of their responsibility to settle the long been outstanding cash advances. 

If the settlement is still not received despite several notifications, coordinate and request to the appropriate Office, for the immediate write-off the long been outstanding cash advances.
observations.reason: No actions were taken

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the identified understatement for the period. Henceforth, strict observance of the guidelines in the proper keeping of accounts is enjoined.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to determine and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the required information in the computation of depreciation such as the date of completion and the appropriate estimated useful life of every asset based on its operation;
Consequently, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute the corresponding accumulated depreciation expense of the above-mentioned accounts and record thereafter in books of account of the LGU.
Henceforth, observe inter-office coordination and communication for a cohesive and organized record-keeping of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
For existing road networks, direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in the determination of the fair value of road lot component for all local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

We recommended Management to strictly adhere to the applicable provisions in Section 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and ensure that appropriations will be disbursed only for specific purposes authorized under the appropriation ordinance.

We recommended Management to immediately stop the granting of cash advance for a special purpose to officials and employees who are neither designated disbursing officer nor bonded with the Bureau of the Treasury. Moving forward, ensure that all cash advances for special purposes are granted to the Municipal Treasurer or a bonded disbursing officer.

We recommended that Management adhere to the procurement process in the implementation of programs and activities to address the COVID-19 provided in RA 9184 and GPPB Circular 01-2020, and discourage granting cash advances to disbursing officers for the procurement of goods in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended Management to strengthen controls over the procurement of drugs and medicines by ensuring that all the necessary documentation are being asked for and complied with to assure that acquisitions thereof concur to be of the best quality, clinically-proven effective and safe for consumption.

We recommended that Management strengthen its monitoring controls by requiring the personnel in-charge of donations to:
Ensure issuance of Acknowledgment Receipts to all future receipts of donations in-kind to confirm that donations are properly recorded and accounted for;
Ensure that all future issuances of donated items are properly supported with Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any document/record to prove receipt by and distribution to the intended beneficiaries; and
Determine the inventory of remaining undistributed items for recording in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to:
Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and
Ensure the one-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

We recommended the Management to:
Require the contractors of 3 infrastructure project to post warranty security to guarantee their performance in the event of eventual structural defects/failures; and
Henceforth, strictly observe and enforce the provisions required by RA 9184 and its IRR specifically its requirements on the posting of warranty securities.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 on timely submission of copies of Contracts/Purchase Orders and supporting documents to the COA Office; and
Notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance so that timely inspection can be undertaken.
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the disbursement vouchers be submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials and employees to submit and post immediately the AAPSI for CY 2019. Henceforth, require the prompt submission of AAPSI and post in the agency’s website within sixty (60) days from receipt of the subsequent Annual Audit Reports (AAR) as provided under the general provisions of succeeding General Appropriations Act.

We recommended that Management direct the:

Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant.

Municipal Accountant to post adjustments in the books of accounts that corresponds to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at every end of the calendar year.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Review all journal entries to ensure that transactions are properly recorded to reflect in the financial statements the actual results of operation for every calendar year; and

Ensure that only actual and valid expenses shall be taken up in the books using the appropriate expenses accounts and be recorded in the period to which these expenses relate.

observations.reason: On-going process of preparing, updating and reconciling the lease contract

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to require the MEE Administrator to:

Instruct the Slaughterhouse Collector to collect slaughter fees based on the rates prescribed under 2012 Revenue Code of Hinatuan; and 

Coordinate with the stakeholders and appropriate office of the LGU to formulate plans in updating/ amending the rate of slaughter fees.
observations.reason: Management already initiated actions, yet awaiting for the final approval

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to require the MEE Administrator to:

Re-assess the actual rates collected by the Market Collector based on the actual number of square meters to be indicated in the sketch map/layout of the Public Market and Terminal.

Ensure that internal controls are put in place in all the operations of the local economic enterprises to safeguard their assets from loss or wastage.
observations.reason: Management already initiated actions, yet awaiting for the final approval

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the MEE Administrator to strictly impose the collection of 25% surcharges for the late payments of the Monthly Rental Fees of the Public Market, Terminal and Enchanted River Stalls, moving forward.
observations.reason: Management already initiated actions, yet awaiting for the final approval

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to require the MEE Administrator to strictly impose the prompt collection of the balances from the delinquent stallholders, and if the delinquent stallholders failed to pay after due notifications, revoke the contract of lease, in consonance with the 2012 Revenue Code of Hinatuan.
observations.reason: Management already initiated actions, yet awaiting for the final approval

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, with coordination with appropriate Office, to ensure that local tax rates are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the Local Government Code, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Management already initiated actions, yet awaiting for the final approval

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned Offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Management already initiated actions, yet awaiting for the final approval

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly observe the provisions of Section 1 of Rule VI of the IRR of Republic Act 9679.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take-home pay for government employees to boost the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.
We recommended that Management require the LDRRM Officer to install a mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PAPs identified in the DRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation.

We recommended that Management:

Fast-track the procurement of the necessary materials and equipment of the two (2) unimplemented projects, otherwise, require the Focal Person to secure written approval from the DOLE-RO for possible changes or deviations from the approved project proposal.  

Instruct the Focal Person to undertake proper and regular monitoring of DOLE-Funded Projects and immediately address the problems that it may encounter.

In the succeeding submission of project proposals, require the Focal Person to thoroughly review the project cost breakdown portion of the proposal and ensure that the identified materials and equipment are available and properly estimated.

We recommended Management to:

Require the Municipal Accountant, Property Officer, Municipal Treasurer to fast-track the reconciliation of their records and thereafter, ensure that these balances are reconciled at year-end.

Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or designated personnel to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets following the pertinent guidelines in supply and property management;  

Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that properties are properly recorded and capitalized, thereafter, apply appropriate systematic depreciation of assets.

We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare the necessary entries to adjust the misclassified balances from Due to Other NGAs to Due to Other GOCCs Accounts. 

Gather the necessary documents related to the PDAF Funded Projects and cause the appropriate actions and remit the same to the National Treasury.

Strengthen the monitoring controls over the utilization of the transferred funds to prevent juggling of funds from different trust fund projects.

Analyze each subsidiary ledger of the Due to Other NGAs Account and identify balances related to the completed projects. Subsequently, prepare the necessary liquidation reports and submit to the respective Source Agencies.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant.
Municipal Accountant to post adjustments in the books of accounts that corresponds to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at every end of the calendar year.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Review all journal entries to ensure that transactions are properly recorded to reflect in the financial statements the actual results of operation for every calendar year; and
Ensure that only actual and valid expenses shall be taken up in the books using the appropriate expenses accounts and be recorded in the period to which these expenses relate.

We recommended that Management require the MEE Administrator to:
Instruct the Slaughterhouse Collector to collect slaughter fees based on the rates prescribed under 2012 Revenue Code of Hinatuan; and
Coordinate with the stakeholders and appropriate office of the LGU to formulate plans in updating/amending the rate of slaughter fees.

We recommended Management to require the MEE Administrator to:
Re-assess the actual rates collected by the Market Collector based on the actual number of square meters to be indicated in the sketch map/layout of the Public Market and Terminal.
Ensure that internal controls are put in place in all the operations of the local economic enterprises to safeguard their assets from loss or wastage.

We recommended Management to require the MEE Administrator to strictly impose the collection of 25% surcharges for the late payments of the Monthly Rental Fees of the Public Market, Terminal and Enchanted River Stalls, moving forward.

We recommend Management to require the MEE Administrator to strictly impose the prompt collection of the balances from the delinquent stallholders, and if the delinquent stallholders failed to pay after due notifications, revoke the contract of lease, in consonance with the 2012 Revenue Code of Hinatuan.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, with coordination with appropriate Office, to ensure that local tax rates are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the  Local Government Code, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned Offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to expedite the processing of transfer of title of lands in favor of the Municipality to avoid any future land disputes; and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all future acquisitions of land are properly supported with necessary documents.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Issue demand letters to notify the concerned Officials and Employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If the settlement was not received, considering that they have been notified, withhold from their salaries the whole amount of the cash advance; and
Strictly monitor the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof and to avoid an undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Issue demand letters to notify the concerned Officials and Employees of their responsibility to settle the long been outstanding cash advances.
If the settlement is still not received despite several notifications, coordinate and request to the appropriate Office, for the immediate write-off the long been outstanding cash advances.
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly observe the provisions of Section 1 of Rule VI of the IRR of Republic Act 9679.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take-home pay for government employees to boost the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended that Management:

Require the MSWDO to attach the required supporting documents enumerated in Section IX of DSWD AO No. 01 series of 2011, together with its certificate of eligibility for the financial assistance for AICS Program before forwarding the same to the Municipal Accountant;

Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the succeeding disbursement vouchers for financial assistance pertaining to AICS Program are supported with complete documents.

Instruct the appropriate Office of the Municipality to establish clear-cut policy and determine an appropriate amount to be granted consistently as burial assistance.

We recommended that Management:

Instruct the HRMO- Designate to require the JO/COS to submit Tax Identification Number (TIN) and Annual Registration Fee (ARF) upon hiring and renewal of contracts, respectively. 

Require the Municipal Accountant to compute, withhold and remit the respective percentage taxes of JO/COS Personnel for CY 2018, as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

Capacitate the Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to attend seminars and trainings related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR Rulings.

We recommended that Management ensure that the items/expenses to be funded by the 20% Development Fund are compliant with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01, in order that the envisioned socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes shall be achieved.

We recommended that Management fast track the development of the LGU Hinatuan’s website. Subsequently, post in the website the vital information of LGU, such as financial reports, infrastructure projects, and transparency seal, among others, in compliance with the provisions of GAA of 2018 and RA 9184.
We recommended that Management require the LDRRM Officer to install a mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PAPs identified in the DRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation.

We recommended that Management:

Fast-track the procurement of the necessary materials and equipment of the two (2) unimplemented projects, otherwise, require the Focal Person to secure written approval from the DOLE-RO for possible changes or deviations from the approved project proposal.  

Instruct the Focal Person to undertake proper and regular monitoring of DOLE-Funded Projects and immediately address the problems that it may encounter.

In the succeeding submission of project proposals, require the Focal Person to thoroughly review the project cost breakdown portion of the proposal and ensure that the identified materials and equipment are available and properly estimated.

We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to include the Early Childhood Care and Development programs in the formulation of SEF Budget in the ensuing year to promote optimum growth and development of our young children and families.

We recommended Management to:

Require the Municipal Accountant, Property Officer, Municipal Treasurer to fast-track the reconciliation of their records and thereafter, ensure that these balances are reconciled at year-end.

Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or designated personnel to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and cause the immediate disposal of unserviceable assets following the pertinent guidelines in supply and property management;  

Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that properties are properly recorded and capitalized, thereafter, apply appropriate systematic depreciation of assets.

We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. 	Prepare the necessary entries to adjust the misclassified balances from Due to Other NGAs to Due to Other GOCCs Accounts. 

b. 	Gather the necessary documents related to the PDAF Funded Projects and cause the appropriate actions and remit the same to the National Treasury.

c. 	Strengthen the monitoring controls over the utilization of the transferred funds to prevent juggling of funds from different trust fund projects.

d. 	Analyze each subsidiary ledger of the Due to Other NGAs Account and identify balances related to the completed projects. Subsequently, prepare the necessary liquidation reports and submit to the respective Source Agencies.

We recommended that Management instruct its Municipal Accountant to specifically identify the composition of the Buildings and Other PPE. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory to this properties to reconcile with the records of the Municipal Accountant and request for the appraisal of these properties to determine its appraised value as of the reporting date. In addition, instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records to all of the LGU’s PPEs and ensure that balances in the subsidiary ledgers reconciled with the balances reported in its appropriate general ledger accounts.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management take appropriate action for the collection of this loans receivables and instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the necessary procedures for the writing off of this account to present fairly the Receivables account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management proposed for write-off
observations.reason: Loans Receivable balance still outstanding as of Dec 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct its Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of its breeding stocks, and prepare and/or update the acknowledgment receipt for property. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to affect any adjusting entries to its Breeding Stocks based on the physical inventory of the Inventory Committee to fairly present the account in its financial statements. On the other hand, prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the LGU and the intended beneficiaries for control and monitoring purposes to prevent further losses of government properties and non-achievement of the program’s goals and objectives.
observations.reason: No Physical Inventory Report submitted and record shows no adjustments made

Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting entry recognizing the completed infrastructure assets to its appropriate asset accounts in the General Fund books. In addition, reconcile the total cost incurred reported under the Status Report of Government Projects and effect any adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Some completed projects are not yet recorded to its appropriate asset account

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to its Inventory accounts. In addition, instruct the Municipal Treasurer and its LEE managers to religiously prepare and submit monthly the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to fairly present its inventory accounts fairly in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to present the current and non-current portion of its Loans Payable in the face of the LGU’s financial statements, and provide information of its amortization in the Notes to the Financial Statements. In addition, the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balance of its Loans Payable with the amortization schedule prepared by the MDFO.
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entry to the appropriate accounts to fairly present the financial statements of the municipality.
We recommended that Management strictly observe the utilization of the 20% Development Fund pursuant to DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.reason: Some ineligible expenditures are reiterated in CY2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) strictly observe the guidelines on the programming and utilization of unexpended LDRRM Fund of previous years to optimally and efficiently utilize the Fund to programs and projects that will enhance disaster-resiliency of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office to maximize the utilization of its LDRRM funds, and to fully implement the programs, projects and activities for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery to achieve the intended purpose of the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (1)review and update its Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan particularly on the disposal of the stored residual and special wastes in the eco-park; (2) prepare and submit an annual progress report on the strategies implemented and accomplishments to the NSWM through its Secretariat as required under NSWMC Resolution No. 486 series of 2016; (3) maintain a monitoring mechanism to provide an on-going check on the effectiveness of the program, to institute measures to improve its performance, and to attain its objective of a clean and wastes-free environment; and (4) prepare the Annual ESWM Plan indicating all activities needed to efficiently and effectively perform its functions with corresponding budget per activity.

We recommended that Management establish the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and allocate the necessary appropriation for the implementation of the programs, projects and activities for the protection of children which is equivalent to 1% of the LGUs Internal Revenue Allotment.
observations.reason: Attributions were made to support LCPC programs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board strictly comply with Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 01 Series of 2017 in identifying programs, projects and activities chargeable against Special Education Fund, and strictly observe the guidelines in the utilization of the Fund.

We recommended that Management instruct the manager of the Local Economic Enterprise to prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each local economic enterprise maintained and operated by the local government unit as basis for the operationalization of the annual operating budget of each LEEs to ensure that programs, activities and projects will contribute to its effective and efficient operation and the achievement of the LEEs goals and objectives.
observations.reason: The preparation is on-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines on the setting up of a Local Economic Enterprise in order to achieve the objectives of an effective and efficient local economic enterprise.
observations.reason: The LGU Pension House is no longer classified as Local Economic Enterprise.  However, the place is used to accommodate guests/visitors to save hotel or lodging expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management establish its Project Monitoring Team to closely monitor the progress of the implementation of every infrastructure projects particularly those that are NGA-funded. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to judiciously prepare the project implementation schedule and to strictly observe its timelines. Lastly, evaluate if implementation “By Administration” is the most economical and efficient practice and/or allowed under the MOA or the program itself for implementing NGA-funded infrastructure projects which would result to the timely completion of the projects and the achievement of the programs’ goals and objectives and the timely enjoyment of benefits by its intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Implementation of NGA funded projects were done “By Contract”.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to meticulously examine the completeness of documents supporting the payment of payrolls for job order and contract of service personnel.  In addition, the management to assess the need of hiring job order and contract of service personnel based on the necessity of their services before issuing the certificate that these services cannot be provided by regular or permanent personnel.
observations.reason: Payrolls of JOs and COS personnel were already supported with Accomplishment Reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions of Section 1 of Rule VI of the IRR of Republic Act 9679.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take home pay for government employees to boast the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.
observations.reason: Loan applications are monitored upon renewal

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management encourage its GAD Focal Point System members to attend the necessary capacity development related to GAD and provide its allocation to the GAD Plan and Budget, and instruct to establish a systematic GAD database to formulate effective and efficient programs, activities and projects under its Gender and Development pursuant to the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and related issuances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct its GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to prepare work and financial plan and/or project implementation schedule for each PPAs to closely monitor the progress of its implementation, and to fast-track the completion of the implemented PPAs. In addition, the GFPS ensure that change of PPAs under its approved GPB be duly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG Provincial Office which would result to the achievement of its objectives addressing gender issues within the municipality.

observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories. The Inventory Committee submitted its report to the MT and reconciled to that of the Municipal Accountant. Intensive inventory on buildings and other structures were not conducted due to insufficiency of records.
observations.reason: The LGU submitted its RPCPPE only for small items. Land, buildings and other fixed structures were not conducted with the physical count. The PPE records submitted were incomplete as to the details for each PPEs.

observations.recommendation: 1.6.    We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting entry recognizing the completed infrastructure assets to its appropriate asset accounts in the General Fund books. In addition, reconcile the total cost incurred reported under the Status Report of Government Projects and effect any adjusting entries.

2.7.   We recommended that Management instruct its Municipal Accountant to specifically identify the composition of the Buildings and Other PPE. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory to this properties to reconcile with the records of the Municipal Accountant and request for the appraisal of these properties to determine its appraised value as of the reporting date. In addition, instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records to all of the LGU’s PPEs and ensure that balances in the subsidiary ledgers reconciled with the balances reported in its appropriate general ledger accounts.

3.7.	We recommended that Management take appropriate action for the collection   of this loans receivables and instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the necessary procedures for the writing off of this account to present fairly the Receivables account in the financial statements.

4.7.	We recommended that Management instruct its Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of its breeding stocks, and prepare and/or update the acknowledgment receipt for property. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to affect any adjusting entries to its breeding stocks based on the physical inventory of the Inventory Committee to fairly present the account in its financial statements. On the other hand, prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the LGU and the intended beneficiaries for control and monitoring purposes to prevent further losses of government properties and non-achievement of the program’s goals and objectives.

5.6.	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to its Inventory accounts. In addition, instruct the Municipal Treasurer and its LEE managers to religiously prepare and submit monthly the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes to fairly present its inventory accounts fairly in the financial statements.

6.8.      We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to present the current and non-current portion of its Loans Payable in the face of the LGU’s financial statements and provide information of its amortization in the Notes to the Financial Statements. In addition, the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balance of its Loans Payable with the amortization schedule prepared by the MDFO.

7.5.      We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entry to the appropriate accounts to fairly present the financial statements of the municipality.
8.8.	We recommended that Management strictly observe the utilization of the 20% Development Fund pursuant to DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

9.10.	We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) strictly observe the guidelines on the programming and utilization of unexpended LDRRM Fund of previous years to optimally and efficiently utilize the Fund to programs and projects that will enhance disaster-resiliency of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office to maximize the utilization of its LDRRM funds and to fully implement the programs, projects and activities for disaster preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery to achieve the intended purpose of the fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board strictly comply with RA 9003 and NSWMC Resolution No. 486 series of 2016.

We recommendeded that Management establish the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and allocate the necessary appropriation for the implementation of the programs, projects and activities for the protection of children which is equivalent to 1% of the LGUs Internal Revenue Allotment.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly comply with Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 Series of 2017 in identifying programs, projects and activities chargeable against Special Education Fund, and strictly observe the guidelines in the utilization of the Fund.

We recommended that Management instruct the manager of the Local Economic Enterprise to prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each local economic enterprise maintained and operated by the local government unit as basis for the operationalization of the annual operating budget of each LEEs to ensure that programs, activities and projects will contribute to its effective and efficient operation and the achievement of the LEEs goals and objectives.

We recommended that Management maintain a separate account and Annual Operating Budget for each Local Economic Enterprises to determine its feasibility and take appropriate measures to improve its operation.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines on the setting up of a Local Economic Enterprise in order to achieve the objectives of an effective and efficient local economic enterprise.

We recommended that Management establish its Project Monitoring Team to closely monitor the progress of the implementation of infrastructure projects particularly those that are nationally funded. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to judiciously prepare the project implementation schedule and to strictly observe its timelines. Lastly, evaluate if implementation “By Administration” is the most economical and efficient practice and/or allowed under the MOA or the program itself for implementing NGA-funded infrastructure projects which would result to the timely completion of the projects and the achievement of the programs’ goals and objectives and the timely enjoyment of benefits by its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management instruct the PESO Manager to prepare the work and financial plan and the project implementation schedule for each DOLE-funded livelihood projects in accordance with the timeframe provided in the MOA, and to fast-track its implementation to achieve the projects’ objectives and the timely enjoyment of benefits by its intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to meticulously examine the completeness of documents supporting the payment of payrolls for job order and contract of service personnel.  In addition, the Management to assess the need of hiring job order and contract of service personnel based on the necessity of their services before issuing the certificate that these services cannot be provided by regular or permanent personnel.
20.5.   We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions of Section 1 of Rule VI of the IRR of Republic Act 9679.

21.6.	We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take home pay for government employees to boast the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended that Management encourage its GAD Focal Point System members to attend the necessary capacity development related to GAD and provide its allocation to the GAD Plan and Budget, and instruct to establish a systematic GAD database to formulate effective and efficient programs, activities and projects under its Gender and Development pursuant to the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and related issuances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct its GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to prepare work and financial plan and/or project implementation schedule for each PPAs to closely monitor the progress of its implementation, and to fast-track the completion of the implemented PPAs. In addition, the GFPS ensure that change of PPAs under its approved GPB be duly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG Provincial Office which would result to the achievement of its objectives addressing gender issues within the municipality.

Require the created Project Inspectorate Team to inspect the reported completed projects, and prepare the necessary documents to be submitted to the office of the Municipal Accountant; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to recognize the completed projects to its appropriate asset accounts in the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded on-going constructed assets to CIP account both in the GF and TF.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant recorded on-going projects to CIP accounts however, these accounts included amounts for completed projects which shall be recorded to appropriate PPE account in the GF.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the collections and remittances of the revenue collectors and instruct the revenue collectors to remit their collections daily and intact;

The Revenue Collectors to report and record promptly their collections and remittances on their cashbook
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already instructed the revenue collectors.
observations.reason: Result of the conduct of cash examination revealed that collections remitted daily except for the collections from Enchanted River due to security reasons.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort in updating the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Tax Payer’s Index Card, respectively.  

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable based on the submitted Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records.
observations.reason: The LGU adopted a system generated report however it was still incompleted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local School Board, in the preparation of the School Board Budget, give priority to programs, activities and projects in accordance to Section 100(c) of Republic Act 7160.  Moreover, stop charging administrative expenses of the District Office to the Special Education Fund (SEF).
observations.managementAction: The LSB was instructed to utilize SEF to PPAs allowed under the program.
observations.reason: There are still expenditures charged against the SEF which are not eligible to be charged against the Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) observe the guidelines in formulating programs, projects and activities to be funded out of the LDRRMF.  Furthermore, the LDRRMC to strictly abide with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and refrain from incurring and charging expenses not related to disaster risk reduction and management activities to LDRRM fund.
observations.reason: The Management prevented the incurrence of expenditures which are not allowable to be charged against the Fund.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant fully transfer the remaining unexpended LDRRM fund for the CY2015 to the Special Trust Fund Account;

The LDRRMC to prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for the current year incorporating therewith the utilization of previous years’ unexpended balances of LDRRMF for proper monitoring on the utilization of the LDRRM fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant transferred the unexpended balance to the Special Trust Fund
observations.reason: Unexpended balance was transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balance of unexpended LDRRMF from  Due to LGUs account to the Trust Liability-DRRM account to present fairly the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant draw adjusting entry for the error noted
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant reclassified the unexpended balance from Due to LGUs account Trust Fund Liabilities

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials are enjoined to follow strictly the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.managementAction: The Management partially implemented this observation.
observations.reason: Counterpart to KALAHI CIDSS was transferred to the TF but was used to pay non-eligible expenditures to the Fund.

observations.recommendation:
Local Development Council and offices concerned to be specific in the formulation of development projects otherwise, ensure that all implemented projects described in generic terms are duly supported with SB resolution in compliance to Section 22 (c) of the Republic Act No. 7160 and COA Memorandum No. 2010-14; 

The Municipal Budget and Municipal Accountant ensure that approved SB Resolutions are submitted before processing disbursement of funds charged against the appropriations of PPAs stated in generic terms.
observations.managementAction: Approval from the Local Sanggunian were required before charges against the PPAs in generic terms will be approved.

observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, PPAs for the current year was either partially and/or not implemented.

observations.recommendation:
The Inspectorate Team meticulously inspect the specifications of the delivered items per delivery receipt and approved purchase order and refrain from accepting items which do not conform with the specifications;

The Management either require the replacement of the product to conform with the specifications and/or rescind the contract and impose liquidated damages against the supplier whichever is appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The Management issued warning to suppliers delivering items not in accordance with specifications.
observations.reason: Suppliers who habitually failed to deliver the items strictly with its specifications were warned and/or issued with blacklisting order and the posting of performance security.

observations.recommendation:
Any BAC members refrain from signing Notices of Award and Purchase Orders for it is contrary to their function as BAC, and 

Management to strictly observe the implementing rules and regulations of RA No. 9184 specifically on the awarding of contracts to winning bidders.
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the BAC not to approve any contract documents for approval by the LCE.
observations.reason: The HOPE or his duly authorized representative was the one signing the said documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) of all the fund transfers received from various National Government agencies to determine the compliance of the terms and conditions of the MOA and to validate if the objectives and expected output were met.
observations.managementAction: The Management the focal offices to forward the MOA for each projects.
observations.reason: MOA were forwarded to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.managementAction: The LGU installed system to mainstream the PPMP and APP with its every offices.
observations.reason: Preparation of PPMP was lodged to the BAC Secretariat instead to each end-users units.

observations.managementAction: The Management installed system to mainstream the preparation and purchase of goods
observations.reason: The LGU installed system on the preparation of PPMP and APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of a GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management used the input from CBMS
observations.reason: GAD Database not yet fully completed. The LGU uses the input from the CBMS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Instruct the Inventory Committee to fully conduct the physical inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment owned by the LGU, including its fixed assets; reconcile the RPCPPE with the Accounting records and effect adjustment, if appropriate;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the RPCPPE on its prescribed format which shall include the actual status of the items of PPE; maintain and renew all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) with the concerned accountable officer and employees reflected in the RPCPPE; and to prepare an inventory of unserviceable properties for appropriate action; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the PPE accounts; and to prepare an adjusting entry to drop from the books properties which could not be located and the unserviceable PPE items.

We recommended that Management:
Require the created Project Inspectorate Team to inspect the reported completed projects, and prepare the necessary documents to be submitted to the office of the Municipal Accountant; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to recognize the completed projects to its appropriate asset accounts in the General Fund books.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort in updating the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Tax Payer’s Index Card, respectively.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to set-up the Real Property Tax Receivable based on the submitted Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the local School Board, in the preparation of the School Board Budget, give priority to programs, activities and projects in accordance to Section 100(c) of Republic Act 7160.  Moreover, stop charging administrative expenses of the District Office to the Special Education Fund (SEF).

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) observe the guidelines in formulating programs, projects and activities to be funded out of the LDRRMF.  Furthermore, the LDRRMC to strictly abide with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 and refrain from incurring and charging expenses not related to disaster risk reduction and management activities to LDRRM fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balance of unexpended LDRRMF from  Due to LGUs account to the Trust Liability-DRRM account to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommended that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials are enjoined to follow strictly the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that:
Any BAC members refrain from signing Notices of Award and Purchase Orders for it is contrary to their function as BAC, and
Management strictly observe the implementing rules and regulations of RA No. 9184 specifically on the awarding of contracts to winning bidders.

We recommended that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) of all the fund transfers received from various National Government agencies to determine the compliance of the terms and conditions of the MOA and to validate if the objectives and expected output were met.

We recommended that:
The end-user offices meticulously and judiciously prepare the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) in accordance with Section 7.3.2 of the RIRR of the RA No. 9184 to avoid delay in the implementation of programs and projects;
The BAC Secretariat and the BAC Chairperson meticulously check the PPMPs prepared by the end-user units before consolidating it to the APP to facilitate the efficient conduct of procurement activities for every procurement project; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure that PPMPs prepared by the end-user units were in accordance to the RIRR of the RA No. 9184 before certifying the correctness of the said Plan.

We recommended that:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly observe the revised implementing rules and regulations of the Procurement Law;
The BAC Secretariat promptly prepare the minutes of meeting of the Committee in accordance to the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and
The Technical Working Group (TWG) meticulously verify and validate the eligibility  requirement of the bidder who was declared as the Lowest Calculated Bidder, and to ensure that additional requirements were submitted before declaring the bidder as the Lowest Calculated and Responsive Bidder.

We recommended that Management require the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of a GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the Municipality.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all fund transfers in the books as receivables to enable the LGU to evaluate whether the funds being transferred is utilized for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassified the fund transfer under the Other Receivables Account.  Demand was sent to the Unit 1 Athletic Association to liquidate the said fund
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, liquidation was fully made.  The LGU School Board prepared its own budget for their participation to athletic meeting & stopped the transfer of funds to their District 1 Athletic Association.

observations.recommendation: Strictly observe the guidelines in the utilization of the Special Education Fund (SEF)provided under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 and 01-A.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer & Municipal Accountant instructed the LSB to stop charging ineligible expenditures.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, ineligible expenditures though minimal, were still charged to the SEF appropriation

observations.recommendation: Strictly adhere to the guidelines in payment of BAC honoraria provided under DBM Circular 2004-5A as amended by DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3 and the Accountant to submit the required supporting documents for such payments.
observations.managementAction: BAC & TWG members were not paid their honorarium until full documentation were attached to their claim.
observations.reason: The claims for honorarium were incompletely supported with supporting documents

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of charging to the 20% development fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials are enjoined to follow strictly the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.reason: For CY2016, the LGU incurred, though minimal, ineligible expenditures to the 20% Development Fund.  See observation No. 2017-002 for full explanation

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) as one of the basic document supporting fund transfer and to record all fund transfers in the books as receivables.  Further require the League to submit liquidation/utilization report to monitor the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management issued office order instructing the Municipal Accountant to ensure that a Memorandum was supported for fund transfer made to leagues.
observations.reason: The LGU executed MOA for fund transfers made to leagues and recorded the same under the “Receivable” Account.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Health Officer to adhere to the provisions of PHIC Circular No. 10 series of 2012 in the disposition of the Per Family Payment Rate Fund and to stop the practice of paying incentives to personnel who are not connected with the delivery of health services.
observations.managementAction: The Management ordered to stop paying incentives to non-health staffs & volunteers.
observations.reason: The payment of incentives were only given to health staff & volunteers either professional or not as long as they are assigned to RHUs.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the OIC College President to refund the unexpended balance of cash advance and to strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 for future procurements.
observations.managementAction: The school in-charge was instructed to liquidate the said cash advance.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the cash advance was partially liquidated.  Procurement of books was procured in accordance to the RIRR of RA9184.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice paying rental on their own heavy equipment utilized in implementing its projects.  Instruct also the Municipal Engineer to exclude the provisions for rental of heavy equipment in the preparation of program of works for future infrastructure projects of the LGU undertaken by administration.
observations.managementAction: The Management instructed to stop the inclusion of heavy equipment rental  if the projects was implemented “By Administration”
observations.reason: The LGU stops the collection of heavy equipment rental if the projects are implemented “By Administration.” It was also verified that equipment rental was not included in the preparation of POW to avoid bloating of the total project cost.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Project In-Charge to determine the personnel/labor requirement for a particular programs/ projects, execute a job order and/or labor pakyaw agreement and require the submission of accomplishment reports from personnel hired on job order basis. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer should ensure that job order contract is executed and submitted before processing the payments for labor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to ensure the completeness of supporting documents to all the claims for disbursements.
observations.reason: For CY2016, the accomplishment reports were attached for claims of wages.

observations.recommendation: Directs the Municipal Accountant to submit promptly all paid DVs, financial reports and supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and the Municipal Treasurer to forward the DVs to the Accounting Office as soon as payments have been made to ensure timely submission of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to promptly submit the financial reports and accounts
observations.reason: For CY2016, the LGU most of the time, still fails to submit their financial reports promptly.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of recognizing deposits in the books without actual deposit of collection in order to show accurate financial information.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was advised to stop the practice to present fairly the financial statements of the LGU

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to render overtime services and to prioritize the reconciliation of the PPE accounts in order to submit the required RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive instructed the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a physical inventory in all PPE owned by the LGU
observations.reason: For CY2016, the LGU conducted a physical count but only to various small equipment and non-fixed assets.  See Observation No. 2017-014 for full discussion.
